perturbation.original_text: Calculation Amount: The lesser of (a) USD 20,000,000.00 and (b) the aggregate Principal Balance (as defined in the Pooling and Servicing Agreement) of the Class I-A-22 Certificates as of the last day of the relevant Calculation Period.
perturbation.changed_text: Calculation Amount: The lesser of (a) USD 20,000,000.00 and (b) the aggregate Principal Balance (as defined in the Pooling and Servicing Agreement) of the Class I-A-22 Certificates as of the first day of the relevant Calculation Period.
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines the Calculation Amount based on the aggregate Principal Balance as of the 'last day' of the Calculation Period. By changing it to 'first day', we create an in-text contradiction regarding when the Principal Balance is assessed, thus affecting the Calculation Amount. This affects the risk exposure calculation for UBS AG.
perturbation.contradicted_text: General Terms Trade Date: 27 April 2006 Effective Date 01 April 2006 Termination Date: 1 April 2009 Calculation Amount: The lesser of (a) USD 20,000,000.00 and (b) the aggregate Principal Balance (as defined in the Pooling and Servicing Agreement) of the Class I-A-22 Certificates as of the first day of the relevant Calculation Period. Seller of the Cap: UBS AG Buyer of the Cap: Counterparty Calculation Agent: UBS AG Business Days: New York Broker: None Fixed Amounts Fixed Rate Payer: Counterparty Fixed Amount: USD[ ] Fixed Rate Payer Payment Date: 27 April 2006 Business Day Convention: Not Applicable Floating Amounts Floating Rate Payer: UBS AG Cap Rate: 5.0 percent per annum Floating Amount: To be determined in accordance with the following formula: Greater of (1)Calculation Amount * Floating Rate Day Count Fraction * (Floating Rate Option - Cap Rate) and (2) 0 Floating Rate Option: USD-LIBOR-BBA Designated Maturity: One Month Spread: None Floating Rate Day Count 30/360 Fraction: Floating Rate Payer Period 01 January, 01 February, 01 March, 01 April, End Dates: 01 May, 01 June, 01 July, 01 August, 01 September, 01 October, 01 November and 01 December, in each year, from and including 01 May 2006, up to and including the Termination Date, subject to adjustment in accordance with the Business DayConvention specified immediately below, and thereshall be No Adjustment to the Period End Dates. Floating Rate Payer Payment Delayed Payment shall be applicable. The Dates: FloatingRate Payer Payment Dates shall be two Business Days prior to 25 January, 25 February, 25 March, 25 April, 25 May, 25 June, 25 July, 25 August, 25 September, 25 October, 25 November and 25 December, in each year, from and including 25 May 2006, up to and including 25 April 2009, notwithstanding the specified &bbsp; Termination Date, subject to adjustment in accordance with the Business Day Convention specified immediately below. Reset Dates: First day of each Calculation Period. Business Day Convention: Modified Following
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies the Calculation Amount is based on the aggregate Principal Balance as of the 'last day' of the relevant Calculation Period. The changed text modifies this to the 'first day'. The contradicted text, which could be the original text before the change, also uses 'first day' to define the Calculation Amount. However, the change explanation highlights that altering the point of assessment (from last day to first day) directly impacts the Calculation Amount. The change therefore makes the initial text contradictory to the changed text, because now it has two different terms. A reasonable reader would recognize the sections as incompatible and unclear because it creates an in-text contradiction regarding when the Principal Balance is assessed, thus affecting the Calculation Amount. This affects the risk exposure calculation for UBS AG.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the terms of Sections 5 and 6 of the Agreement, if Counterparty has satisfied all of its payment obligations under Section 2(a)(i) of the Agreement with respect to this Transaction, and unless UBS AG is required to return (whether pursuant to an order of a court with due authority to cause UBS AG to be required to return any such payment to Counterparty (or any duly authorized representative thereof) or whether otherwise pursuant to appropriate proceedings to return to Counterparty (or any duly authorized representative thereof)) or UBS AG otherwise returns to Counterparty (or any duly authorized representative thereof) upon demand of Counterparty (or any duly authorized representative thereof) any portion of such payment, then: (a) the occurrence of an event described in Section 5(a) of the Agreement with respect to Counterparty shall not constitute an Event of Default or Potential Event of Default with respect to Counterparty as the Defaulting Party in respect of this Transaction and (b) UBS AG shall be entitled to designate an Early Termination Date pursuant to Section 6 of the Agreement in respect of this Transaction only as a result of a Termination Event set forth in either Section 5(b)(i) or Section 5(b)(ii) of the Agreement with respect to UBS AG as the Affected Party or Section 5(b)(iii) of the Agreement with respect to UBS AG as the Burdened Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the terms of Sections 5 and 6 of the Agreement, if Counterparty has satisfied all of its payment obligations under Section 2(a)(i) of the Agreement with respect to this Transaction, and UBS AG is required to return (whether pursuant to an order of a court with due authority to cause UBS AG to be required to return any such payment to Counterparty (or any duly authorized representative thereof) or whether otherwise pursuant to appropriate proceedings to return to Counterparty (or any duly authorized representative thereof)) or UBS AG otherwise returns to Counterparty (or any duly authorized representative thereof) upon demand of Counterparty (or any duly authorized representative thereof) any portion of such payment, then: (a) the occurrence of an event described in Section 5(a) of the Agreement with respect to Counterparty shall constitute an Event of Default or Potential Event of Default with respect to Counterparty as the Defaulting Party in respect of this Transaction and (b) UBS AG shall be entitled to designate an Early Termination Date pursuant to Section 6 of the Agreement in respect of this Transaction regardless of a Termination Event set forth in either Section 5(b)(i) or Section 5(b)(ii) of the Agreement with respect to UBS AG as the Affected Party or Section 5(b)(iii) of the Agreement with respect to UBS AG as the Burdened Party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'unless' at the start of the second condition and modifying subsections (a) and (b), we change the relationship between Counterparty's payment obligations and the consequences of an event described in Section 5(a) or UBS AG's right to designate an Early Termination Date. The original text provides protections to the Counterparty if they've met obligations. The new version removes such protections, meaning an event of default can be called upon even if the counterparty has met payment obligations, and changes the circumstances under which UBS AG can call an early termination date, thus creating a material in-text contradiction that unfairly benefits UBS AG.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (xii) Fully Paid Transaction. Notwithstanding the terms of Sections 5 and 6 of the Agreement, if Counterparty has satisfied all of its payment obligations under Section 2(a)(i) of the Agreement with respect to this Transaction, and UBS AG is required to return (whether pursuant to an order of a court with due authority to cause UBS AG to be required to return any such payment to Counterparty (or any duly authorized representative thereof) or whether otherwise pursuant to appropriate proceedings to return to Counterparty (or any duly authorized representative thereof)) or UBS AG otherwise returns to Counterparty (or any duly authorized representative thereof) upon demand of Counterparty (or any duly authorized representative thereof) any portion of such payment, then: (a) the occurrence of an event described in Section 5(a) of the Agreement with respect to Counterparty shall constitute an Event of Default or Potential Event of Default with respect to Counterparty as the Defaulting Party in respect of this Transaction and (b) UBS AG shall be entitled to designate an Early Termination Date pursuant to Section 6 of the Agreement in respect of this Transaction regardless of a Termination Event set forth in either Section 5(b)(i) or Section 5(b)(ii) of the Agreement with respect to UBS AG as the Affected Party or Section 5(b)(iii) of the Agreement with respect to UBS AG as the Burdened Party. For purposes of the Transaction to which this Confirmation relates, Counterparty's only obligation under Section 2(a)(i) of the Agreement is to pay the Fixed Amount on the Fixed Rate Payer Payment Date, each as defined in this Confirmation.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly alters the conditions under which Counterparty is protected from events of default and early termination. The original text states that *unless* UBS AG is required to return payment, certain protections apply to the Counterparty. The changed text removes 'unless,' effectively nullifying these protections. The contradicted text, which is identical to the changed text, reflects this new, less favorable condition for the Counterparty, directly conflicting with the protections initially afforded, and therefore establishes a strong in-text contradiction. The new version of the agreement would now allow UBS AG to call an event of default or early termination, even if the Counterparty has met their payment obligations under 2(a)(i), regardless of Termination Events set forth in 5(b)(i) or 5(b)(ii) or 5(b)(iii), which benefits UBS AG.

perturbation.original_text: Each party constitutes an "eligible contract participant" as such term is defined in Section 1(a)12 of the Commodity Exchange Act, as amended.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party does not constitute an "eligible contract participant" as such term is defined in Section 1(a)12 of the Commodity Exchange Act, as amended.
perturbation.explanation: The parties are representing to each other they are an "eligible contract participant." If they are not, then this contract cannot exist. If one party is an "eligible contract participant" and the other is not, it is not possible to form the interest rate cap transaction. This introduces an in-text contradiction that voids the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (d) Eligible Contract Participant. Each party does not constitute an "eligible contract participant" as such term is defined in Section 1(a)12 of the Commodity Exchange Act, as amended.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that "Each party constitutes an 'eligible contract participant'", while the changed text states that "Each party does not constitute an 'eligible contract participant'". The contradicted text also states that "Each party does not constitute an 'eligible contract participant'". The original text and the contradicted text are already contradictory on their own, however the change emphasizes that these parties are not eligible contract participants, which in turn highlights the contradiction. Since the contradicted text states the same thing as the Changed Text, it might seem like there is no contradiction. However, because the original text directly opposed what the contradicted text stated, there is a clear contradiction. As stated in the Perturbation Explanation, if the parties are not eligible contract participants, the contract cannot exist, meaning there is a fundamental contradiction.
